codeReview.summary: The patch fixes top-level function-generic lookup and now typechecks default trait bodies, but it still does not carry declared trait bounds from outer impl/trait generic parameters into method-body checking, so the original generic-bound method-resolution failure is not fully resolved.

issues.description: `check_fn_decl` builds `param_bounds` from `fd.fn_generics` only, while the enclosing `ItemImpl`, `ItemTraitImpl`, and `ItemTrait` environments at lines 1507, 1527, and 1630 add outer generic names to `type_params` without seeding matching bounds. Because bounded generics are supported on impls and traits (`lib/parser.mly` generics in impl/trait productions and `lib/ast.ml` item definitions), a method or default body that uses an outer bounded parameter like `impl<T: Show> Box<T> { fn render(item: T) -> str { item.show() } }` still sees `T` as unbounded and rejects a valid trait call. That leaves the Phase 8 fix incomplete relative to the original blocking review finding that generic receiver method lookup must honor the receiver type parameter's declared trait bounds.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `compiler-worker` skill still says compiler features should extend tests before implementation, but this worker edited `lib/typecheck.ml` and built/tested the change before later adding the regression cases in `test/test_typecheck.ml`; the handoff still reports `followedProcedure: true` while also recording that deviation.

summary: Review failed: commit `b43379d` fixes the original function-level bounded-generic lookup bug and the default-trait-body gap, but it still drops bounds declared on outer impl/trait generics, so the original Phase 8 blocking issue remains unresolved.
